title: With the inauguration of Governor Gavin Newsom’s second term to take place this morning, the California Senate Republican Caucus shares a list of the governor’s top 10 failures tha
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: California is home to America’s most homeless people despite spending more than $20 billion of taxpayer dollars under Governor Newsom's leadership.
California lacks the water storage capacity to capture water from atmospheric river storms, impacting farmers and farmworkers.
Governor Newsom's proposed 'Windfall Profits Tax' on the gasoline industry would increase the cost of gasoline at the pump.
Two-thirds of California students failed to meet Math standards according to statewide test scores.
Stanford University Hoover Institution Study reveals that businesses are leaving California twice as fast as in previous years.
Governor Newsom's Employment Development Department sent out $33 billion in unemployment fraud during the disorganized effort to provide benefits.
An investigation found that Governor Newsom overstated the number of acres treated with fuel breaks and prescribed burns by 690%.
The actual number of acres treated as a result of Governor Newsom's executive order was only 11,399 acres, contrary to his claim of 90,000 acres.
Governor Newsom has been criticized for his inconsistent stance on the High-Speed Rail project, leading to significant taxpayer expenditures.
